slots.meaning: The second-person protagonist's role aboard the crossing.
slots.guidance: A single unnamed role title for the second-person protagonist; capable, non-reckless, non-violent.

slots.meaning: The type of craft the hero pilots across the strait for the whole run.
slots.guidance: A small, fast, deniable craft suited to a night crossing under a blockade; no weapon imagery.

slots.meaning: The named body of water the whole run crosses; the story's single bounded, hazardous space.
slots.guidance: A single named strait/channel/crossing the story never leaves; no toxic or unbreathable air as a hazard.

slots.meaning: The delivered goods that give the run its purpose (named at the two successful/completion endings).
slots.guidance: A single benign, carriable delivered good or document; never a weapon, never graphic.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 1 of 13 (a harbor barrier at the mouth of the strait).
slots.guidance: A physical barrier obstacle with a narrow legitimate way through; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 2 of 13 (a marked field of true/false channel markers).
slots.guidance: A field of markers/signs, some genuine and some decoys; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 3 of 13 (a patrolling guard vessel's fixed lane).
slots.guidance: A patrolled lane or route with a predictable, timeable turn; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 4 of 13 (a sweeping searchlight over open water).
slots.guidance: A sweeping detection beam/scanner with a readable rhythm; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 5 of 13 (a listening post that hears engine noise).
slots.guidance: A sound-based detection post; defeated by quiet, not violence; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 6 of 13 (a field of moored charges with a narrow clear lane).
slots.guidance: A hazard field with one narrow safe lane through it; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself (the deterministic 'lethal'/'weapon' half of the mine hazard belongs to the fixed, unslotted fail-branch prose, not this slot).

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 7 of 13 (a grounded wreck with one safe channel around it).
slots.guidance: A grounded/sunken obstruction with one scoured safe channel; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 8 of 13 (a shore battery's ranged, rhythmic barrage).
slots.guidance: A ranged, rhythmic bombardment pattern with a safe lull; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 9 of 13 (a fog bank crossed by held compass course).
slots.guidance: A concealing natural hazard crossed by discipline (a held course), not brute force; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 10 of 13 (a spaced line of lamp-signalling guard boats).
slots.guidance: A spaced line of watch-posts with a dark gap between them; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 11 of 13 (an inner boom patrolled by a warden's skiff).
slots.guidance: A second barrier patrolled by a single roving guard craft on a fixed round; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 12 of 13 (a breaking tidal bar with a timed safe slot).
slots.guidance: A natural water hazard crossed on correct timing, not force; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: The named hazard/checkpoint identity at station 13 of 13 (the final wired landing beach with one cleared lane).
slots.guidance: A staked barrier belt on the landing itself, with one legitimate cleared lane; no toxic hazard as the obstacle itself.

slots.meaning: Ending title: the full, silent success where both hero and cargo cross clean (fixed SUCCESS ending).
slots.guidance: A short, quietly triumphant title naming the clean-run ending; positive, non-violent.

slots.meaning: Ending title: a harder, battered but successful completion of the run (fixed COMPLETION ending).
slots.guidance: A short title naming a hard-won completion; battered but through, never graphic.

slots.meaning: Ending title: the cargo is surrendered but the hero survives; a spent, non-triumphant completion (fixed COMPLETION ending).
slots.guidance: A short, wry, quiet title naming survival without the prize.
